califOrnia
Fair Political
Practices Cornmission
Jayne F. Hernan
Deputy County Counsel
1195 Third Street, Roon 301
Napa, CA 94559-3001
January 6, 1992
Re:
Your Request for Informal Assistance
our File No. I -91-5 57
Dear LIs. Herman:
This letter is in response to your request for advice
regarding the duties and respons ibi I ities of Tony Holzhauer under
ttre cont i ict-of- interest provisions of the PoIj'tical Reform Act
(the "Act").1 
Your request is general in nature and you have
provided us vrith insufficient infornation for us to determine
,nheth"r Mr. Holzhauer's economic interests will- be affected
materially by the pending decisions. Accordingly, we are
providing you wi.th- inforrnal assistance pursuant to the provisions
of negufition 18329.2
OUESTI ON
Do the conf I ict-of - interest provisions of the Act require
Commissj-oner Tony Holzhauer to disqualify himself fron
participating in decisions regarding an inclusionary housing
ordinance which would encourage the provision of affordable
housing in connection vrith the develoPment of residential
proj ects?
1 cor.rn*"nt code Sections 81ooo-91015. AIl statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. 
Comrnission
regulations appear at 2 caLifornia code of Regulations Section
I-8OOO, et seo. AII references to reguJ.ations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California code of Regulations'
2 Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Section 83114,' Regulation r.8329 (c) . )
428 J Street, Suite 8OO o P.O. Box 8O7 o Sacramento CA 958O4-O8O7 o (916)322.5660


Our Fi■e No. I-9■-557
Page 2
CONCLUSION
The conf I ict-of- interest provisions of the Act require
Commissioner Tony Holzhauer to disqualify hirnself from
participating in decisions regarding the proposed ordinance if it
is reasonably foreseeable that the pending governmental decisions
wiII have a naterial financial- effect on his economic interests
which is distinguishable frorn the effect of the decisions on the
public generall-y, as dj.scussed belolr.
FACTS
Tony Holzhauer is a commissioner on the Napa County
Conservation, Developnent and Planning Corunission. Comrnissioner
Holzhauer is currently employed as a real estate agent for Up
Val1ey Associates (trUp ValJ.eyrr), a real estate f irrn located in the
City of St. Helena, County of Napa. As a cornmissioned agent for
Up VaIley, Comnissioner Holzhauer currently has several listings
in the unincorporated areas of Napa County, but has earned no
conrnissions in the past tlrelve nonths from the sale of properties
located in unincorporated areas of Napa County. Commissioner
Holzhauer may earn such cornmissions in the future should a
favorable opportunity present itself.
The housing elenent of the Napa County general plan states
that the provision of affordable housing in the county is to be
encouraged in connection $rith the developnent of resi&ential
projects. 
The Napa County Conservation, Developnent and planninq
Commission is currently scheduled to consider whether to recommend
to the Napa County Board of Supervisors that an inclusionary
housing ordinance be developed and adopted as part of a
comprehensive response to thj.s aspect of the county,s general
plan. such a reconmendation, if rnade, could include endorsement
of a model ordinance requiring sone or all of the following: 
(1)
that a certain percentage of aII new residentj.al units conitructed
in a project be "affordable[ as that terrn is defined in the
ordinance; (2) that a fee be paid to an affordable housing fund
in lieu of the actual construction of affordable units; or (3)
that construction of a certain nunber of affordable units take
place off-site.
In addition, the Napa County Conservation, Development and
Planning Cornmission is likely to reconmend the creation of
additionaL sources of funding for affordable housing through neans
such as assessnent of linkage fees fron developers of
agricultural, 
commercial and industrial projecls based upon
proj ect-generated employment, inposition of-a business 1-icense
tax, or increase in docurnentary transfer or sales taxes. Shoutd
cornrnissioner Holzhauer ever becone invorved in the narketing of a
property located in an unincorporated area of Napa county, iuch
property would be subject to any ordinance ultirnitely adopted by
the county of Napa Board of Supervisors.


Our Fi■e No. I-9■-557
Page 3
3  An officia■′s llimmediate familylt are his or her spouse and
dependent children.  (SeCtion 82029。
)
ANALYS I S
Section 87100 prohibits pub).ic officials fron making,
participating in, or using their official position to j-nf I'uence a
governrnental decision in which they knor., or have reason to kno$/
fhey have a financial interest. 
Cornmissioner Holzhauer is a
public official. 
(Section 82048. )
Financial Interests
An official 
has a financial interest in a decision within the
rneaning of section 87100 if it is reasonably foreseeable . that the
decisi5n will have a material financial effect, distinguishable
frorn its effect on the public generally, on the official, 
or on a
;;il"; 
of the official's 
imnediate tamily,3 or on:
(a) Any business entity in which the public
official 
has a direct or indirect investrnent worth
one thousand dollars (S1,000) or nore.
(b) Any real property in which the public
official 
has a direct or indirect interest $,orth
one thousand dollars ($1,00o) or nore'
(c) Any source of income, other than gifts
and other thin loans by a conmercial lending
institution 
in the regular course of business on
terms available to the Public without regard to
official 
status, aggregating two hundred fifty
dolfars ($250) or nore in value provided to,
received by or promised to the public official
within 12 months Prior to the time when the
decision is made.
(d) Any business entity in which the public
official 
is a director, officer, partner, trustee,
ernployee, or holds any position of management.
(e) Any donor of, or any interrnediary or
agent for a donor of, a gift or gifts aggregating
two hundred fifty dollars ($250) or nore in value
provided to, received by, or prornised to the public
otficia:. within 12 nonths prior to the tirne when
the decision is made.
For purposes of this section, indirect
investnent or interest neans any investrnent or
interest ovrned by the spouse or dependent child of
a public officj.al, by an agent on behalf of a


Our File No. 
■-9■-557
Page 4
public official, 
or by a business entity or trust
in which the official, 
the official's 
agents,
spouse, and dependent children own directly,
indirectly, 
or beneficially a 1o-percent interest
or greater.
Section 87103 .
Cornmissioner Holzhauer has a nurnber of potentially
disqualifying economic interests hlhich must be evaluated to
determine whether he must disqualify himself fron participating in
governmental decisions regarding the proposed ordinance.
Comrnissioner Holzhauer has an econonic interest in Up VaIley, the
conpany $rhere he is enployed, pursuant to Section 87103(d). In
aaaition, commissioner Holzhauer has an economic interest in
clients fron whom he has received connission incorne of $250 or
more in the preceding tlrelve nonths. Regulation 18704.3 provides
that the following are deemed to be sources of incone to a real
estate agent within the rneaning of subdivision (c) of Section
87103:
(A) The broker and brokerage business entity
under lrhose auspices the agent works;
(B) The person the agent represents in the
transaction,' and
(c) Any person who receives a finder's or
other referral fee for referrinq a party to the
transaction to the broker, or who makes a referral
pursuant to a contract lrith the broker-
Regulation 1.8704.3 (c) (3) .
Accordingly, Conrnissioner Holzhauer must consider the
financial effect of the pending decisions on the following
economic interests: 
(1) Up valley Associates which is the broker
and brokerage business where commissioner Holzhauer is employed as
a comnissioned real estate agent; (2) any person who has been a
source of comrnission incone to colunissioner Holzhauer of S250 or
more in the preceding 12 months; and (3) any person who has
received a fee for referrinq a party to a transaction to Up vall'ey
Associates. consequently, Conuissioner Holzhauer must determine
the foreseeable and naterial financial effect of decisions
regarding the proposed ordinance on each of these economic
interests to determine whether he rnust disqualify hiroself fron
participating in such decisions.
Makinq. Participatino in Makinq. or Attemptinq to Influence a
Governmental Deci-sion
A pubLic official 
rnakes a governnental decision or
participates in the naking of a governmental decision whenever the


Our File No. I-9■
-557
Page 5
public official 
votes on a matter, commits the agency to a course
of action, or enters into any contractual agreement on behalf of
the agency. (Regulation 18700(b).) AdditionalLy, a public
official 
participates in a governmental decision when, acting
within the authority of his or her position, the public official:
(1) Negotiates, without significant
substantive review, with a governmentaJ. entity or
private person regarding the decision,. or
(2) Advises or makes reconmendations to the
decision-maker, either directly or without
significant intervening substantive review, by:
(A) Conducting research or making any
investigation which requires the exercise of
judgnent on the part of the official 
or
designated enployee and the purpose of which
is to influence the decision; or
(B) Preparing or presenting any report,
analysis or opinion, oraIly or in writing,
which reguires the exercise of judgrment on the
part of the official or designated enployee
and the purpose of which is to influence the
decision.
Regulation 18700 (c) .
With regard to a governrnental decision which is within or
before an official's 
agency or an agency appointed by or subject
to the budgetary control of his or her agency, an officiaL is
attempting to use his or her official position to influence the
decision if, for the purpose of influencing the decision, the
official 
contacts, or appears before, or otherwise attempts to
influence any nenber, officer, enployee or consultant of the
agency. Attempts to influence include, but are not limited to,
appearances or contacts by the official 
on behalf of a business
entity, client, or custoner. (Regulation 18700.1.)
Accordingly, if it is reasonably foreseeable that the
economic interests of Cornmissioner Holzhauer will be materially
affected by a governmental decision, he must not only disquaLify
hinself from participating in formal decisions of the Napa County
Conservation, Development and PLanning Conmission which may affect
such interests, but he rnust also abstain from attenpting to
influence such decisions by comnunicating with other nenbers of
the commission or the staff regarding the decisions.


Our File No. I-9■-557
Page 6
Foreseeabilitv
The effect of a decision is reasonably foreseeable if there
is a substantial likelihood that it $riII occur. To be
foreseeable, the effect of a decision must be more than a nere
possibility,' however, certainty is not required. 
(DovJnev cares v.
Downey Communitv Development conn. (1987) 196 CaI.APP.3d 983, 989-
991; Witt v. I'lorrow (1,977) 70 CaI.App.3d 817; In re Thorner (1975)
1 FPPC Ops. 198.) The Act seeks to prevent more than actual
conflicts of interest; it seeks to prevent even the appearance of
a possible conf lict of interest. 
(witt v. U.9.EE9!, supra at 823. )
It is foreseeable that the proposed ordinance will have an
effect on comnissioner Holzhauer' econonic interests. 
The
ordinance pertains to the developnent of affordable housing in the
county of Napa where both Up valley Associates and Comrnissioner
Holzhauer are engaged in the sale of real property. In general
terms, affordable housing prices tend to be lohler thus generating
Lor.rer sales commissions for the real estate agents hrho represent
buyers and sell.ers in transactions j'nvolving such homes. on the
other hand, nore affordable housing nay result in increased sales
and, consequently, increased conrnissions. AdditionaIly, the
proposed ordinance would result in greater cost to developers
engaged in residentiaL construction within the county thus.
inireasing the sale price of hones and Potentially generating
higher commission for sales agents. Presumably, irnposing.
afiordable housing requirements on residential or commercial
developers also wiII lead to a greater stock of houses for sale,
thus generating the potential for nore com:nission incorne.
accordingly, the foreseeability el'ement is met. we now deterrnine
whether thE effect of the pending decisions on co:nnissioner
Holzhauer's economic interests wiII be rnaterial.
Materia I itv
Regulation 18702 sets forth the guidelines for deter:nininq
whether an official's 
econornic interests are rnaterially affected
by a decision. If the official's 
econonic interests are directlv
invol.ved in the decision, ReguJ.ation L8702.1 provides the
appropriate standard for assessing materiality. 
However, when the
official's 
economic interests will be affected indirectlv by a
decision, the appropriate standards for deternining rnateriality
are those of Requlations 18702.2 throuqh 18702.6. These
regulations are discussed below.
A. Economic Interests Directlv Involved in the Decision
A person or business entity is directly involved in a
decision before an official's 
agency when that person or entity,
either personally or by an agent:
(1) Initiates the proceeding in which the
decision will be rnade by filing an application,
claim, appeal, or sirnilar reguest or;


Our Flle NO. I-9■
-557
Page 7
(2') Is a named party in, or is the subject
of, the proceeding concerning the decision before
the official 
or the official's 
agency.
(3) A person or business entity is the
subject of a proceeding if a decision involves the
issuance, renewal, approval, denial or revocation
of any license, permit, or other entitlement to, or
contract with, the subject person or business
ent ity .
Regulation 18702. 1(b) .
It does not appear that conmissioner Holzhauer's econornic
interests wiLl be directty involved when the commission considers
the proposed ordinance and affordable housing issues. Therefore,
the appropriate standards for deterrnining materiality are those
discussed be1ow,
B. Econornic Interests Indirectlv Involved in the Decision
1. Up vallev Associates
Conmissioner Holzhauer is an employee of Up Valley
Associates, a business entity engaged in the sale of real estate
in the County of Napa. This business entity is also a source of
income to comnissioner Holzhauer. Presunably, Cornnissioner
Holzhauer has received $250 or nore fron Up vaIley Associates in
the preceding t$relve months. This business entity will 
be
affelted indirectlv by decisions regarding the proposed ordinance.
when an official 
has an economic interest in a business entity
indirectly involved in the decision, the apPropriate standard for
deternining materiality is that of Regulation L8702.2. For a
business such as Up valtey Associates, the applicable standards
are those of Subdivision (g) of Regulation 18702.2 which states
that the effect of a decision upon a business entity indirectly
involved in a decision is material if:
(1) The decision will result in an increase
or decrease in the gross revenues for a fiscal year
of S10,000 or nore; or
(2) The decision wiII result in the business
entity incurring or avoiding additional expenses or
reducing or eliminating existing expenses for a
fiscal year j.n the amount of 52,500 or more; or


Our File No. I-9■
-557
Page 8
(3) The decision will result in an increase
or decrease in the value of assets or liabilities
of S10,000 or more.
ReguJ.ation L87 02 .2 (g) .
If a decision will affect a business entj,ty in which an
official 
has an econornic interest in the amounts set forth above,
the effect of the decision is rnaterial. we have insufficient
i.nforrnation to make this deternination. Cornmissioner Holzhauer
nust assess the potentiaL effect of decisions regardinq the
proposed requirenents for affordable housinq or in lieu fees upon
tnis uusineJs entity which is a source of incorne to hirn. Anong
the factors to be considered in naking this evaluation is whether
the decisions wilI result in decreased or increased revenues to
this business as the requirements irnposed by the proposed
ordinance may result in an increase in the housing stock available
for resale oi, conversely, reduce the average resale price of
homes as more affordable housing is built in the unincorporated
areas of the county. If Cornmissioner Holzhauer determines that
the effect of such decisions upon UP Valley Associates wiII be
rnaterial, he must disqualify hirnself frorn participating in such
decisions.
2. Sources of Cornmission fncorne
Cornrnissioner Holzhauer has received commission lncome from
Dersons he has represented in real estate transactions. 
Any
personl who has blen a source of cornrnission incone to comnissioner
i{o}zhauer of S25o or more in the preceding twelve rnonths is
potentially a disqualifying financial interest-
In addition, if conmissioner Holzhauer is acting as an agent
for the sa1e, purchase, or lease of real property, he rnay, in
certain c j-rcurnstances, be the recipient of "promised'r income
within the rneaning of Subdivision (c) of section 87103.
conmission income is deemed "promised" incorne to a real estate
agent when the sale is pending (i.e., the sale is in escrow) .
(iarsen Advice Letter, No. A-82-192; Armento Advice Letter, No. I-
90-o32. )
You have stated in your request for advice that commissioner
HoLzhauer has not received cornrnission income for sales in the
unincorporated areas of the county in the preceding t$relve rnonths.
However, a source of incone located in the city of St. Helena nay
have econonic interests in the unincorporated areas of the county
r^rhich may be naterially affected by the pending decisions. If any
4 "Person' means an individual, proprietorship, firn,
partnership, joint venture, syndicate, business trust, company,
corporation, association, committee, and any other organization or
group of persons acting in concert. (section 82047.)


our File No. 
■-9■-557
Page 9
I
of the sources of income nentioned above hlill be naterialty
affected by decj,sions regarding the proPosed ordinance,
comrnissionlr Hol,zhauer must disqualify hirnself from participating
in such decisions. The standard for determining rnateriality with
regard to an individual who is a source of incone of $250 or more
to a public official 
in the twelve months preceding a decision and
who is indirectly involved in a governmental decision is that of
Regulation \a702.6 which states that:
The effect of a decision is material as to an
individual who is a source of income or gifts to an
official 
if any of the following applies: 
i
I
(a) The decision wilL affect the individuat'i
incone, investments, or other tangible or
intangible assets or liabilities 
(other than real
property) by 51,000 or more; or
(b) The decision wilI affect the individual's
real property interest in a nanner that is
considered rnaterial under Section 18702.3 or
Section r8702.4.
Regulation 18702.65.
Please note, however, that if the source of income is a
business entity indirectly involved in the decJ-sion, then the
applicable standards for deterrnining materiality are those of
nllulation L8702.2 discussed above with reference to Up valley
Associates.
Public Genera I lv
Even if the reasonably foreseeable financial effect of a
governnental decision is material, disqual if ication is required
only if the effect is distinguishable fron the effect on the
pultic generally. 
(section 8?100.) If the decision does not
affect all members of the public in the sane manner,
disqualification 
may be required unless the effect of the decision
on the public official's 
econonic interests is the same as the
effect on a signj.ficant segrnent of the public. 
(Regulation
18703.) For purposes of our discussion, the public is the entire
population of the county. (In re Leqan (1985) 9 FPPC ops' 1; fn
re owen (1976) 2 FPPC ops.77i Joroensen Advice Letter, No. A-90-
017. )
5
and Enclosed for your reference are copies of Regulations 18702.3
14702.4.

we conclude that the rrpublic Aenerallytr exception is
inapplicable to commissioner Holzhauer. This is so because his
economic interests are peculiarly tied to the sale of real estate
and the proposed ordinance nay have an impact on the housing stock
within the county which is available for sale and on the sale
value of such housing stock.
we trust this letter adequately responds to your inquiry.
Should you have any further questions regarding this matter, do
not hesitate to call ne at (916) 322-59OL.6
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
